rules_applied.RULE7: FIRES — SCO win 13.06% < 15%. Submit unshaded model estimates. RULE15 applies for McTominay (Q9) — BUT survival match context overrides standard suppression (Scotland MUST attack).
rules_applied.RULE15: Near-applicable for McTominay (SCO 13.06% win). HOWEVER: survival match overrides. Scotland MUST attack aggressively. Do NOT fully suppress McTominay SOT. Use ~50% vs the 0.25-0.35 range that strict RULE15 would impose.
rules_applied.FLOOR_0.25: Applied to Q2 (Scotland 2+ offsides) — computed ~17%, floor at 0.25 adjusted upward to 0.32 for survival attacking context.
rules_applied.RULE_FOULS: Not the primary driver here. Scotland fouls while defending AND attacking (21 fouls in GD1). Brazil more disciplined. Foul direction unclear.
rules_applied.SURVIVAL_MATCH_SCOTLAND: Scotland MUST WIN or DRAW to advance with highest confidence. They will attack aggressively, push men forward, and leave space for Brazil counter-attacks. This elevates: Brazil goals in 2H, total goals, BRA corner count.

group_context.group: Group C — Brazil, Morocco, Scotland, Haiti

gd3_stakes.Scotland: WIN = automatic knockout qualification. DRAW = very likely through as best 3rd-place (Opta 73.57% odds with draw). LOSS = depends on scoreline and Morocco-Haiti result. SURVIVAL MATCH.
gd3_stakes.Brazil: WIN = group winner. DRAW = qualified. Raphinha OUT. Can afford a draw but want to win.
gd3_stakes.simultaneous: Morocco vs Haiti playing at same time.

SCO_GD1_vs_Haiti.mctominay_gd1: Shot hit post/crossbar at 17' — NOT on target. 0 confirmed SOT.

SCO_GD2_vs_Morocco.note: Scotland had 0 SOT in 90 minutes — only 2nd time in WC history. McTominay shots at 85'/88' both missed target.
SCO_GD2_vs_Morocco.mctominay_gd2: 2 shot attempts (85', 88') — both off target. 0 SOT. 4 total shots across 2 WC games, 0 SOT.

BRA_GD2_vs_Haiti.bra_goals: Cunha 23'/36' (first Brazilian 1H WC brace since Neymar 2014), Vinicius 45+3'
BRA_GD2_vs_Haiti.critical_note: Brazil had only 2 shots in 2H after going 3-0 up. Zero 2H goals across BOTH WC matches (artifact of leads). But no incentive to push in 2H when up 3-0.

Scott_McTominay.club_2025_26: Napoli (Serie A) — moved from Manchester United summer 2024
Scott_McTominay.position: Box-to-box CM / advanced CM — frequent goalscorer
Scott_McTominay.gd1_notable: Shot hit crossbar/post at 17' — borderline, counted as miss
Scott_McTominay.gd2_notable: 2 shots at 85' and 88' — both missed target. Scotland 0 SOT total.
Scott_McTominay.bet365_sot_market: 11-10 (implied ~52%) — Racing Post flagged as best bet
Scott_McTominay.rule15_assessment: RULE15 technically fires (SCO 13.06% < 15%). BUT McTominay is an advanced CM, not a forward. MORE IMPORTANTLY: Scotland survival match = Scotland MUST attack = he gets forward constantly. Standard RULE15 forward-suppression does NOT apply in survival attacking context. Use ~0.50 (market-aligned).

Vinicius_Jr.gd2_minutes: Subbed off 81' specifically to rest for SCO-BRA
Vinicius_Jr.note: All goals in 1H across WC. Rested in GD2 intentionally. Expected to be fresh and aggressive.

Matheus_Cunha.gd2_detail: Cunha 23' and 36' — first Brazilian 1H WC brace since Neymar vs Cameroon 2014

referee.name: Cesar Ramos
referee.note: 2022 WC semi-final referee (France vs Morocco). High-card disciplinarian.

Q1.question: Will Brazil have more shots on target than Scotland in the second half?
key_drivers: Model: P(BRA more 2H SOT) = 66.68%
Scotland WC avg: 0-1 SOT per match. GD2: 0 SOT total.
Brazil counter-attacks off Scotland's desperate forward pushes
Survival match = Scotland leaves space = more Brazil shots

Q2.question: Will Scotland be caught offside 2 or more times?
key_drivers: Model proxy: P(SCO 2+ offsides | lambda=0.75) = 17.33%
FLOOR_0.25 binds as minimum; adjusted to 0.32 for survival attacking context
Scotland GD2 vs Morocco: 1 offside in full match
Brazil's high defensive line (Marquinhos, Gabriel) = some offside trap risk
Scotland pushing 3+ forwards forward = some runs behind line

Q3.question: Will Scotland score the first goal AND Brazil score in the second half?
key_drivers: Compound question requires both events
P(SCO first scorer): model 28.5%, market-adjusted 20.25%
P(BRA 2H scores): 59.47%
FLOOR_0.25 does NOT apply to compound scenario questions
MAJOR NO EDGE: crowd likely ~0.22; we're at 0.13

Q4.question: Will Brazil finish with more corner kicks than Scotland?
key_drivers: Model: P(BRA more corners full match) = 82.04%
Brazil GD1 vs Morocco: 6 corners vs Morocco's 2
Scotland GD2 vs Morocco: only 2 corners (while Morocco had 5)
Brazil wide attackers Vinicius left / Rayan right create corner pressure
Scotland defending deep = more Brazil territory

Q5.question: Will Scotland score at least 1 goal?
key_drivers: Model: P(SCO scores) = 52.76%
Market (BTTS No -165): P(Scotland scores) implied ~37.5%
Blend with survival match uplift: ~45%
Scotland GD2: 0 SOT in 90 minutes — very poor recent form
RULE7: don't suppress too far below model (submit near-model estimate for extreme underdog)

Q6.question: Will the match have 3 or more total goals?
key_drivers: Model: P(total ≥ 3) = 42.82%
Market: Over 2.5 at -126 to -130 (~56% implied)
Survival match = Scotland attacks open = Brazil counters = more goals
Blend: ~50%. Survival match adjustment: +2pp → 0.52

Q7.question: Will Brazil score in the second half?
key_drivers: Model: P(BRA 2H scores) = 59.47%
Brazil WC record: 0 second-half goals in 2 matches — BUT both were leads (1H: 1-1 vs Morocco, 3-0 vs Haiti → no incentive to attack in 2H)
Against Scotland (who must attack): Brazil will have space to counter in 2H
Vinicius Jr rested from 81' specifically for this match
Situation-adjusted from 59% to 62%

Q8.question: Will there be 4 or more total cards shown?
key_drivers: Ramos career: 4.17 YC/game → P(4+) = 59.9%
Ramos WC average: 4.0 YC/game → P(4+) = 56.7%
Scotland GD1: 4 total cards (3 SCO + 1 HAI) — Scotland physical team
Scotland committed 21 fouls in GD1 — physical approach expected vs Brazil
Survival match = more desperate fouls = more cards

Q9.question: Will Scott McTominay have at least 1 shot on target?
key_drivers: Napoli 2025-26: 33 SOT in 33 games = 1.0 SOT/90 (genuine shooting CM)
WC2026: 4 shots total, 0 confirmed SOT across 2 games
Bet365 prices at 11-10 (~52%) — near even money. Racing Post best bet.
Scotland survival match = McTominay gets forward more than usual
RULE15 technically fires (SCO 13%) BUT survival match overrides standard suppression
WC 0-SOT record and Brazil's quality defense slightly pull estimate below market (0.52 → 0.50)

Q10.question: Will Brazil score in the first half?
key_drivers: Model: P(BRA 1H scores) = 52.24%
Evidence: Brazil scored in 1H in BOTH WC matches (4 first-half goals: 32', 23', 36', 45+3'). 0 second-half goals.
Scotland conceded in the 2nd minute vs Morocco — vulnerable to early pressure
Vinicius Jr fresh after being subbed 81' in GD2
Evidence-adjusted upward from model: 0.62

edge_ranking.note: Compound: SCO first AND BRA 2H. ~12-17% prob. Crowd ~0.22. Major ~9pp NO edge.
Brazil more 2H SOT. Scotland avg 0-1 SOT/game.

questions.question: Will Brazil have more shots on target than Scotland in the second half?
Will Scotland be caught offside 2 or more times?
Will Scotland score the first goal of the game and Brazil score in the second half?
Will Brazil finish with more corner kicks than Scotland?
Will Scotland score at least 1 goal?
Will the match have 3 or more total goals?
Will Brazil score in the second half?
Will there be 4 or more total cards shown?
Will Scott McTominay have at least 1 shot on target?
Will Brazil score in the first half?

key_learnings: BRA more corners (Q4) = NO despite 3-0 win — Brazil won 3-0 but Scotland got more corners. Confirms corner volatility even in dominant wins. Third consecutive data point after MEX-KOR (KOR more corners despite losing) and CZE-MEX (CZE more corners despite losing 0-3). CORNER ANOMALY: losing team's corners are not reliably suppressed.

BRA more 2H SOT (Q1) = NO — Brazil likely scored all 3 goals in 1H and coasted. Scotland in desperate mode pushed forward in 2H, generating more 2H SOT than Brazil. RULE10 analog: in blowouts, chasing team can dominate 2H shooting stats even from losing position.

4+ total cards (Q8) = NO despite Ramos high card rate (4.17 YC/game) — a 3-0 win reduces card incentive. Cards need match tension; a decided blowout = no reason for either side to foul desperately. Our 0.62 was too high.

McTominay SOT (Q9) = YES — survival-match override of RULE15 was fully validated. Scotland's must-win context forced McTominay forward; he delivered a SOT. +10.39 RBP from a coin-flip estimate that beat crowd's 0.43.
Q3 (Scotland first AND Brazil 2H) = NO — extreme NO (0.13) was correct, compound question logic held, beat crowd (0.20) by 7pp. +4.49 RBP.
